item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jfc I live like 20 minutes away from this place. I’ve even stood in front of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a really good one! I agree with Wolfen32, it's really cool when articles can be linked back to real-world locations in a believable way and this entry does a great job with it. I also really like how the story plays out over the course of the addendums, it's a really interesting one. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, I just love when articles are linked back to a real event or place as a way of an alternative explanation for their existence. Perhaps someone could write a Roadside series about the various anomalous attractions of the American West.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’m not sure what you mean by technocratic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I tried to get through this, I liked the ideas at play, but I found the description to be turgid and the interviews un-engaging. I think if you cleaned up a lot of the more technocratic parts of the conprocs and main body, and refined the dialogue in the interviews, I would find it to be more enjoyable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, this is a really good article. It’s long, but it’s not boring by any stretch of the word- I was hooked from beginning to end, and it stops short at just the right point to make me want more. I suppose my only complaint is that it ends, haha. Very well done, this gets a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-5220-1 claims that the object possesses far greater power that remains dormant.


This in itself doesn't really sound clinical and stood out to me against the rest of the article. If this was directly stated by SCP-5220-1 than it should be in quotes. Other than that it's a pretty well thought out article with an interesting idea that didn't draw out too long.
+1

item.title: Re: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Dude, this is amazing! I really enjoyed this SCP.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Well, here it is, my first SCP.

Thanks to the following individuals:

Francis Scalia and Dr. Leibowitz for their help critiquing the initial idea.

JakDragonX for taking my first draft to task, Unclenicolini and DrFullham for helping me refine it.

Image credit: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spiral_Jetty#/media/File:Spiral-jetty-from-rozel-point.png / Wikipedia user Soren.harward, public domain
